div1.entryFree:
Sufficient, 1) adequate to the end proposed, enough: Gent. V, 4, 75. Shr. IV, 4, 45. H5 I, 2, 141. III, 2, 64. H6A II, 3, 56. V, 5, 13. V, 5, 13 H6C I, 3, 26. Tim. III, 5, 61. Cymb. I, 4, 161.
2) fit, able: are there not men in your ward s. to serve it? Meas. II, 1, 281. Meas. II, 1, 281 some s. honest witnesses, Shr. IV, 4, 95. half a dozen s. men, H4B III, 2, 102. you'll never meet a more s. man, Oth. III, 4, 91. all in all s. IV, 1, 276.
In the language of Shylock, == capable of paying, solvent, rich: my meaning in saying he is a good man is to have you understand me that he is s. Merch. I, 3, 17. the man is, nothwithstanding, s. Merch. I, 3, 17
